Content:
What Is Merchant Account Activation?

Merchant Account Activation is the process of reviewing and approving a merchant’s information before their account is enabled to process transactions on Xendit. This process ensures that only legitimate, compliant, and low-risk merchants are activated on the platform.

Account activation is a critical control point that helps protect Xendit, merchants, and end customers from fraud, regulatory breaches, and operational risk.

Purpose of Account Activation

The account activation process aims to:

  • Verify the merchant’s business legitimacy and readiness

  • Confirm the merchant’s legal identity and documentation

  • Assess fraud, scam, and compliance risks before activation

  • Ensure alignment with Xendit’s internal policies and regulatory requirements

Only merchants who successfully pass the activation review will be allowed to process transactions.

High-Level Account Activation Flow

The account activation process generally follows these stages:

  • Merchant submits onboarding details and required documents, including the

  • Initial data and completeness check by MO including Business Due Diligence (BDD) review and Legal Due Diligence (LDD)

  • Activation decision: Activate, Reject, or Request Revision

  • Escalation to stakeholders if issues or risks are identified

Key Review Components

Business Due Diligence & Legal Due Diligence

As part of the merchant onboarding (MO) process, Xendit conducts due diligence to ensure that every merchant onboarded is legitimate, compliant, and operationally ready to process transactions. This due diligence is divided into two main components: Business Due Diligence (BDD) and Legal Due Diligence (LDD).

Together, BDD and LDD help Xendit mitigate risk, maintain regulatory compliance, and ensure a safe and reliable payment ecosystem for both merchants and end customers.

Scope

Definition

Key focus areas

Business Due Diligence

Business Due Diligence (BDD) is the assessment of a merchant’s business platform and customer-facing presence to determine operational readiness and business legitimacy.

Business model clarity, product/service information, pricing transparency, customer communication, and risk indicators.

Legal Due Diligence

Legal Due Diligence (LDD) is the verification of a merchant’s legal identity and documents to ensure compliance with regulatory and internal requirements.

Review of legal documents such as business registration, identification documents, licenses, and supporting records.

Further information regarding the process of Business Due Diligence and Legal Due Diligence, you can find in articles “KYC Flow” based on the regions

Stakeholder & Escalation Owner

The Merchant Onboarding (MO) process involves close collaboration across multiple teams within Xendit. Each stakeholder plays a distinct role in ensuring that merchants are reviewed thoroughly from business, legal, risk, and operational perspectives.

This section outlines the key departments involved in the MO process, along with their respective Points of Contact (PICs) and roles

Departement
